quickStart.title: Get productive in 30 seconds
steps: Install the Cline extension and open its sidebar in VS Code/Cursor/JetBrains-supported environments.
Open settings with the gear icon or Command Palette → 'Cline: Open Settings'.
Pick a provider (Cline, Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenRouter, Google, Ollama, etc.) and authenticate/sign in.
Start in Plan mode for unfamiliar codebases, then switch to Act mode once the approach is solid.
Use @ mentions like @/file, @problems, @terminal, and @git-changes to feed the exact context Cline needs.
Keep approvals tight at first; only expand Auto Approve categories after you trust the workflow.

dailyWorkflows.title: Plan then Act
dailyWorkflows.description: Use Cline's dual-mode workflow to separate exploration from execution on anything non-trivial.
dailyWorkflows.command: Start in Plan mode → inspect codebase/searches → switch to Act mode to implement

dailyWorkflows.title: Debug with targeted context
dailyWorkflows.description: Send the failing files, problems panel, and recent terminal output instead of pasting giant logs manually.
dailyWorkflows.command: @/relevant/file + @problems + @terminal → ask Cline to isolate and fix the issue

dailyWorkflows.title: Run long commands without blocking the task
dailyWorkflows.description: Approve the command, then use Proceed While Running so Cline can keep working while dev servers/tests continue in the background.
dailyWorkflows.command: Approve terminal command → click Proceed While Running when appropriate

dailyWorkflows.title: Use tasks as one-goal work units
dailyWorkflows.description: Keep each task scoped to one objective so context stays relevant and checkpoints remain useful.
dailyWorkflows.command: One task per feature/bug; use /newtask when switching goals

dailyWorkflows.title: Add project conventions through rules
dailyWorkflows.description: Persist coding standards and project constraints in .clinerules instead of retyping them every session.
dailyWorkflows.command: Create .clinerules/*.md or use /newrule to scaffold one

dailyWorkflows.title: Extend Cline with MCP
dailyWorkflows.description: Connect external tools/services so Cline can query APIs, databases, browsers, and internal systems through MCP servers.
dailyWorkflows.command: Open MCP Servers UI → add/configure marketplace, remote, or custom server → approve tool usage

powerMoves.title: Use /deep-planning for high-blast-radius work
powerMoves.description: Let Cline investigate silently, ask clarifying questions, produce implementation_plan.md, and spin a fresh implementation task.
powerMoves.command: /deep-planning Build X across frontend, backend, and infra
powerMoves.why: It front-loads architecture thinking and creates a cleaner execution task for the actual build.

powerMoves.title: Tune Plan and Act to different models
powerMoves.description: Assign a stronger reasoning model to planning and a faster/cheaper model to implementation.
powerMoves.command: Settings → enable different models for Plan and Act → choose per-mode models
powerMoves.why: You spend premium tokens where thinking matters and save money during routine execution.

powerMoves.title: Use checkpoints aggressively
powerMoves.description: Roll back workspace changes without losing the conversation, or compare snapshots to branch an experiment.
powerMoves.command: Use checkpoint Compare/Restore controls during a task
powerMoves.why: Makes exploratory refactors and browser/debug loops much safer.

powerMoves.title: Let Cline build or install the MCP tool it needs
powerMoves.description: Ask Cline to add a custom tool when the task depends on Jira, AWS, PagerDuty, or another external system.
powerMoves.command: 'Add a tool that fetches Jira tickets' / configure MCP server
powerMoves.why: You can turn recurring external dependencies into first-class tools instead of manual copy-paste context.

gotchas.problem: If you keep unrelated work inside one task, the context window fills with noise and output quality drops.
gotchas.solution: Use one task per goal and start a fresh task when changing features or bug lines; /newtask is the built-in handoff path.

gotchas.problem: YOLO Mode disables all approval checks, including commands, file edits, browser actions, MCP tools, and mode transitions.
gotchas.solution: Reserve YOLO for throwaway sandboxes or highly trusted repetitive flows; otherwise use selective Auto Approve categories.

gotchas.problem: Auto Approve toggles are layered; 'all files' and 'all commands' do nothing unless the base project toggle is already on.
gotchas.solution: Enable base categories first, then extend them deliberately if you truly need broader scope.

gotchas.problem: Cline does not ship with MCP servers preinstalled.
gotchas.solution: Plan to source, configure, or build the MCP servers you need before depending on those tools in a workflow.

gotchas.problem: Large irrelevant folders can inflate baseline context usage and cost.
gotchas.solution: Use .clineignore to exclude dependencies, build artifacts, and other junk from the workspace context.

slashCommands.description: Create a fresh task with distilled context from the current conversation.
slashCommands.example: /newtask Continue the auth feature implementation, preserving decisions and next steps.
slashCommands.tip: Use it when the current task is getting noisy or context-heavy but the work should continue.

slashCommands.description: Compress the current conversation while staying in the same task.
slashCommands.tip: Prefer this when you're mid-debugging session and want to keep task identity intact.

slashCommands.description: Interactively create a reusable rule file for Cline preferences or project standards.
slashCommands.example: /newrule Always write API handlers with zod validation and structured error responses.
slashCommands.tip: Great after you notice the same correction being repeated across multiple tasks.

slashCommands.description: Trigger a more thorough planning workflow that investigates the codebase, asks questions, writes implementation_plan.md, and creates an implementation task.
slashCommands.example: /deep-planning Migrate the billing system from REST polling to webhooks.
slashCommands.tip: Best for multi-file features, architectural changes, and high-risk refactors.

slashCommands.description: Generate AI explanations for diffs, commits, branches, PRs, or local changes (VS Code only).
slashCommands.tip: Useful for reviews, onboarding, and understanding what changed before you approve a big patch.

slashCommands.description: Collect diagnostics and prepare a useful bug report for the Cline team.
slashCommands.tip: Run it immediately after reproducing a bug so the diagnostics are still fresh.

config.description: Select the inference backend such as Cline, Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenRouter, Google Gemini, AWS Bedrock, Ollama, and many others.
config.recommended: Start with Cline for easiest setup, Anthropic for dependable tool use, or OpenRouter for cost/model flexibility.

config.description: Choose the active model for the current workflow; Cline supports cloud, local, and OpenAI-compatible providers.
config.recommended: Use a reliable frontier model for complex Act tasks and cheaper/faster options for routine edits.

config.key: Use different models for Plan and Act
config.description: Automatically switch models when you swap between planning and execution modes.
config.recommended: Enable this if you want a stronger planner and a faster executor.

config.description: Granular toggles for reading files, editing files, commands, browser use, MCP use, and notifications.
config.recommended: Start with read-only permissions, then gradually allow edits or safe commands once the project setup is trustworthy.

config.description: Workspace rule directory for persistent markdown instructions; Cline also detects .cursorrules, .windsurfrules, and AGENTS.md.
config.recommended: Split rules by concern (coding, testing, architecture) and keep them short enough to avoid wasting context tokens.

config.description: Exclude files/directories from Cline's effective workspace context.
config.recommended: Ignore node_modules, dist/build output, generated files, vendored code, and other token sinks.

config.description: Configure marketplace, remote, or custom MCP servers to extend Cline with external tools and resources.
config.recommended: Add only the servers you actually need and keep credentials scoped tightly.

proTips: Cline performs best when you give it the right context, not the most context—use @ mentions surgically.
Plan mode is intentionally non-destructive; use it to understand the codebase before spending expensive Act-mode tokens on edits and commands.
Selective Auto Approve is the sweet spot: enough automation to avoid popup fatigue, not enough to let a bad prompt run wild.
Rules are portable: Cline can automatically detect .cursorrules, .windsurfrules, and AGENTS.md, which is handy in mixed-tool teams.
If a task is going long, checkpoint early and often so you can compare or restore workspace state without losing your conversational reasoning trail.

resources.title: Cline Docs: Auto Approve & YOLO Mode
